ab: I dafak helek no lahok ko pitelek kisonan nga’ay to haw?
ch: W:我和您約明天下午好嗎?
en: Can I make an appointment with you tomorrow afternoon?

ab: Nga’ay to, icowa kita a masaso’araw?
ch: M:可以，我們在哪裡見面?
en: Sure, where should we meet?

ab: I pitilidan kita nga’ay to haw? misasing kita.
ch: W:我們約學校好嗎?我們要錄影。
en: How about meeting at school? We need to shoot a video.

ab: Itira i sefi ko nga’ay, ciriko’ kako to no Pangcah.
ch: M:那去聚會所好了，我會穿族服去。
en: Then let’s go to the community center. I’ll wear folk costume.

ab: Tada makapahay ko celek no sakola i niyaro’ ita. ka fitelekan no hana i, tayni sato a misalama!
ch: W: 我們部落的櫻花盛開很漂亮。等花開的時候再來這裡玩!
en: The cherry blossoms in our tribe are beautiful when they bloom. Come find me when the flowers bloom!

ab: Tahira i kacanglahan, halafinay tato!
ch: M: 那要等到春天，太久了吧!
en: That would have to wait until spring. It’s too late, isn’t it?

ab: I ka ilisinan a tayni kiso da!
ch: W: 那你豐年祭的時候來吧!
en: Then come during our Harvest Festival!

ab: Hayda! hinatalaay kako!
ch: M: 好啊!我很期待!
en: Alright! I’m looking forward to it!

ab: Mana mafolaw kiso a tayni i niyaro’?
ch: W: 請問你為甚麼要搬回部落生活?
en: May I ask why you decided to move back to the tribe?

ab: Malasingsi kako a minokay.
ch: M: 我回去要當老師。
en: I want to return to teach at the local school.

ab: Naw caka i tokay a pasifana’?
ch: W: 為甚麼不在都市教書?
en: Why not teach in the city?

ab: Nawhani ma’ilol kako to niyaro’, nanay ira ko pipanocay i niyaro’.
ch: M: 因為我懷念我的故鄉，也希望能回饋部落。
en: Because I miss my hometown and I also want to give back to the tribe.

ab: Mafana’ay kiso a mitenoon, cima ko pasifana’ay tisowanan?
ch: M:你很會織布，是誰教你的?
en: You’re very skilled at weaving, who taught you?

ab: Ci mamo ako, o tada mafana’ay cingra a mitenoon a tamdaw i niyaro’ niyam.
ch: W:是我的奶奶，她是我們部落最會織布的人。
en: It was my grandmother, she is the most skilled weaver in our tribe.

ab: Mana sapi nanamaw kiso a mitenoon?
ch: M:你怎麼會想要學織布?
en: How did you develop an interest in learning weaving?

ab: O serangawan no mita ko tenoon, sapi parayrayaw kako to serangawan.
ch: W:織布是我們的文化，我想要讓文化延續。
en: Weaving is a part of our culture, and I want to ensure its continuity.

ab: Fangcal a ’owaken koni a epah.
ch: M:這個小米酒真好喝。
en: This millet wine is delicious.

ab: Kako ko mieraway yo!
ch: W:是我自己釀的呦!
en: I made it myself!

ab: Tada caloway to kiso, halafin ko pieraw haw?
ch: M:你真厲害，這要釀很久嗎?
en: You’re really talented. Does it take a long time to make?

ab: Caay, cecay a lipay manga’ay to. rahkaden ho ko falinono ato felac, tongalen to otong a mitiyol, ta manga’ay to a telien i koreng.
ch: W:不用，大約一周就好了。把糯米和白米先蒸熟，再加入麴粉一起拌勻，就可以裝到甕裡了。
en: No, it only takes about a week. After steaming the sticky rice and white rice, we mix it with yeast powder, and then it can be stored in a jar.
